matched_codes.fit_note: Russia KLEMS NACE 1.0 C is mining and quarrying; maps to OKVED2 B.
Russia KLEMS NACE 1.0 D is manufacturing; maps to OKVED2 C.
Russia KLEMS NACE 1.0 E combines electricity, gas and water supply; close but not identical to OKVED2 D+E.
Russia KLEMS NACE 1.0 F is construction; maps to OKVED2 F.
Russia KLEMS 60t63 covers transport and storage; it excludes post and telecommunications.
Russia KLEMS 64 covers post and telecommunications. It is only a legacy proxy for OKVED2 information and communication.
Russia KLEMS NACE 1.0 J is financial intermediation; maps to OKVED2 K.

matched_codes.russia_klems_desc: Renting of m&eq and other business activities
matched_codes.fit_note: Russia KLEMS 71t74 mixes renting, computer-related, R&D and other business activities; it is a broad proxy for OKVED2 M and part of J.

aggregation_rules.index_aggregation_note: Mapped sectors are single-code in this version; mean aggregation is a fallback for future multi-code mappings.

proxy_formulas.official_productivity_hours_gap_cagr: CAGR(real VA per worker proxy, 2017-2024). Hours-worked series is unavailable in the current repo, so worker productivity is used as the observed labour-saving proxy.
proxy_formulas.official_capital_labour_services_gap_cag: 0.5 * (CAGR(real VA, 2017-2024) - CAGR(FOT proxy, 2017-2024)) + 0.5 * CAGR(fixed-asset renewal coefficient, 2017-2020).
proxy_formulas.official_ict_nonict_services_gap_cagr: CAGR(0.5 * server_share_pct + 0.5 * website_share_pct, 2017-2024) from Rosstat ICT usage by activity.
proxy_formulas.mos_score_updated: 0.35 * updated_productivity + 0.30 * updated_capital_labour + 0.20 * updated_ICT + 0.15 * updated_employment, where updated component score is a year-span-weighted blend of KLEMS 2000-2016 and official 2017-2024 normalized component scores.

limitations: The proxy does not identify deliberate planned obsolescence or collusion.
It measures sectoral pressure under which firms or regulators may prefer staged deployment, compatibility limits, controlled repair/access, or slower capability release.
Russia KLEMS Release 3 uses NACE 1.0 and covers 1995-2016; mapping to current OKVED2 sectors is approximate for J and M.
Russia KLEMS notes that 2015-2016 values are preliminary and labour shares are fixed at the 2014 level in this release.
Special caution is required for oil and gas related industries because of transfer pricing and cross-industry allocation issues noted by Russia KLEMS.
Rosstat fixed-asset renewal table currently contributes 2017-2020 values; the 2021-2024 tail is not published in the same machine-readable table used here.

Recent official block uses observed VA, employment, wage-bill proxy and ICT-use shares. This is materially better than relying only on 1995-2016 KLEMS, but it remains a proxy layer rather than a direct measure of ICT versus non-ICT capital services.
